---
title: "conda"
author: "AY"
date: '`r format(Sys.time(), "%d %B, %Y")`'
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## Conda guide for this workflow

This page explains how to create and maintain the workflow environment using **conda** commands.

The repository environment is defined in:

- `env/environment.yml`

It includes `snakemake`, aligners (`STAR`, `bwa`, `bowtie2`), and supporting tools (`samtools`, `bedtools`, `curl`, `wget`, etc.).

To learn more about conda, see the [official documentation](https://docs.conda.io/).

## 1) Create the environment

You can create it either by **name** or by **path prefix**.

By name:

```bash
conda env create --name bcfngs-mapping --file env/environment.yml
```

By prefix (useful on shared systems):

```bash
conda env create --prefix ~/miniconda3/envs/update_genomes --file env/environment.yml
```

## 2) Activate the environment

By name:

```bash
conda activate bcfngs-mapping
```

By prefix:

```bash
conda activate ~/miniconda3/envs/update_genomes
```

If activation fails in a fresh shell, initialize conda once:

```bash
conda init
```

Then open a new terminal and activate again.

## 3) Verify the installation

```bash
python --version
snakemake --version
STAR --version
bowtie2-build --version
samtools --version
```

## 4) Update the environment after changes

When `env/environment.yml` is updated, run:

```bash
conda env update --name bcfngs-mapping --file env/environment.yml --prune
```

Or (prefix-based):

```bash
conda env update --prefix ~/miniconda3/envs/update_genomes --file env/environment.yml --prune
```

`--prune` removes packages that are no longer listed in the file.

## 5) Optional: export explicit package lists

For reproducibility snapshots:

```bash
mkdir -p env/locks
conda list --explicit --name bcfngs-mapping > env/locks/explicit-conda-YYYY-MM-DD.txt
```

## Notes

- Create once, then activate on each new login/session.
- Keep `env/environment.yml` as the source of truth.
- In this repository, `./scripts/bcfngs env ...` is implemented with micromamba. This page provides equivalent conda-native commands.
